description: Fix project persistence bug: on initialization via Firestore, load ALL project documents (not just the active project) so that non-active projects survive a page refresh.
steps: In src/persistenceService.js: Add a loadAllProjectsFromFirestore() function that queries all documents from the Firestore projects collection using getDocs.
In src/persistenceService.js: Update initializePersistence() Firestore path to call loadAllProjectsFromFirestore() and populate the projects Map with ALL projects, not just the active one.
In src/persistenceService.js: Hydrate localStorage with ALL project metadata (cm-proj-* keys) from Firestore, not just the active project.

In src/App.jsx: Update the Firestore init path to load ALL project documents from Firestore, reconstruct all projects in firestoreProjects array, while only loading workspace data for the active project.
acceptance_criteria: initializePersistence() Firestore path loads all projects from Firestore into the projects Map
App.jsx Firestore init path loads all projects into firestoreProjects array
Only the active project's workspaces are loaded into memory (performance optimization)
All project metadata is hydrated to localStorage (cm-proj-* keys for each project)
npm run build exits with code 0
verification: Run npm run build in /projects/sandbox/ClearMind and confirm exit code 0
findings: Added loadAllProjectsFromFirestore() function. Fixed both persistenceService.js initializePersistence() and App.jsx Firestore init path to load ALL projects via getDocs on the projects collection. Only the active project's workspaces are loaded into memory. All project metadata is hydrated to localStorage. npm run build passes cleanly.
